title: salvia euphratica var. leiocalycina
intro: Salvia euphratica var. leiocalycina, also known as a variety of the White Toad Tree or Tengger Flower, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Salvia euphratica var. leiocalycina include reducing fever, relieving cough and asthma symptoms, and promoting wound healing, as well as exerting antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Herbal preparations of Salvia euphratica var. leiocalycina are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes, and can be found in various forms in traditional Chinese medicine and herbal remedies.
